title: Orchestrated A/B-Test Anchoring with Fast-Fallback and promotion hooks
preview: Motivation: Teams running A/B tests and canary promotions need deterministic cohort anchoring plus an automated fast-fallback when latency, error-rate, or quality regressions are detected. This PR introduces a controller and API surface that: 1) pins user/query cohorts to a stable treatment (anchor) during long-runn...
text:
Motivation: Teams running A/B tests and canary promotions need deterministic cohort anchoring plus an automated fast-fallback when latency, error-rate, or quality regressions are detected. This PR introduces a controller and API surface that: 1) pins user/query cohorts to a stable treatment (anchor) during long-running A/B experiments, 2) provides promotion hooks to move anchored cohorts from shadow->canary->primary on a schedule or metric gate, and 3) implements a fast-fallback path that immediately drains traffic from a failing variant and re-anchors affected cohorts to the last known-good variant.

Changes include: controller implementation (orchestration/scheduler), a new lightweight cohort store (kv-backed, optional in Dedicated/Private), API surface for experiments (create/update/pause/promote), telemetry and probe instrumentation (per-cohort latency/error/quality counters), and...

title: Reduce CPU serialization overhead and introduce request-affine thread pinning
preview: Motivation: production canaries showed CPU-side stalls in the request path under medium tail-load (concurrent short requests). Root cause analysis indicated three correlated issues: a global serialization lock in the request marshalling path, KV-cache read contention on a small number of shards, and excessive cross-...
text:
Motivation: production canaries showed CPU-side stalls in the request path under medium tail-load (concurrent short requests). Root cause analysis indicated three correlated issues: a global serialization lock in the request marshalling path, KV-cache read contention on a small number of shards, and excessive cross-thread wakeups from the executor when continuous batching is active. This PR addresses those CPU-side bottlenecks with low-risk, togglable changes that reduce serialization overhead and make scheduling request-affine so hot requests avoid context migration.

Summary of changes: I) Replace global request-serialization mutex with per-worker small-buffer fast-path using FlatBuffers for marshalling and a fallback to proto when necessary. II) Introduce a request-affine thread-pool (request-affine executors) that pins a request's lifecycle to a CPU core group to reduce cache chur...

title: handoff: privacy filter and attachment rate-limit proxy
preview: Add a configurable PII redaction pipeline to handoff notes and an ephemeral attachment proxy with per-incident and per-user rate limits. Motivation: customers reported accidental PII leakage in handoff notes and large attachments causing spiky egress and long-term retention burdens. This PR implements a layered appr...
text: Add a configurable PII redaction pipeline to handoff notes and an ephemeral attachment proxy with per-incident and per-user rate limits. Motivation: customers reported accidental PII leakage in handoff notes and large attachments causing spiky egress and long-term retention burdens. This PR implements a layered approach: 1) a lightweight PII classifier + heuristic regex scrubber exposed as internal/handoff/scrubber.go with three redact levels (none, redact-pii, redact-sensitive) and config driven allowlists; 2) an attachment proxy service internal/proxy/attach_proxy.go that accepts multipart uploads, applies size/type heuristics, stores content in an ephemeral blobstore with TTL and returns short access tokens; 3) enforcement of per-incident and per-user upload rate limits via a sliding-window counter stored in Redis with configurable quotas and backoff; 4) telemetry and audit hooks i...

title: Introduce sequential probe compositor and adaptive cutover allocator
preview: Motivation: improve canary probe efficiency and reduce probe-induced noise during rollouts by composing sequential probes and allocating cutover budgets adaptively based on recent telemetry. This change introduces a Sequential Probe Compositor that stitches short-window synthetic probes into prioritized sequences to...
text: Motivation: improve canary probe efficiency and reduce probe-induced noise during rollouts by composing sequential probes and allocating cutover budgets adaptively based on recent telemetry. This change introduces a Sequential Probe Compositor that stitches short-window synthetic probes into prioritized sequences to better surface phase-aligned regressions, and an Adaptive Cutover Allocator that computes per-rollout cutover budgets using traffic-conditioned stability scores and SLO-weighted credits. Key goals: lower false positives from probe collisions, preserve signal fidelity for tail latency, and enable safer gradual cutovers for large traffic shifts. Implementation notes: compositor merges probe windows across latency, throughput and error-rate signals; it maintains ordering and backpressure semantics so probes do not overload healthy replicas; allocator uses EWMA of percentile d...

title: add kernel-hotspot tracer, kvcache offset histogram, and continuous batch pressure probe
preview: Motivation: It has been difficult to correlate kernel-level hotspots with KV cache layout and continuous-batching backpressure events in production traces. This PR introduces three complementary low-overhead probes and a small microbench harness to make cause-and-effect visible during perf investigations and automat...
text: Motivation: It has been difficult to correlate kernel-level hotspots with KV cache layout and continuous-batching backpressure events in production traces. This PR introduces three complementary low-overhead probes and a small microbench harness to make cause-and-effect visible during perf investigations and automated regressions. Summary of changes: (1) kernel-hotspot-tracer: lightweight sampling probe that annotates scheduler and CUDA kernel entry/exit with a compact hotspot identifier derived from callsite and op-type. Emits NVTX ranges when available and falls back to short tracepoints for environments without NVTX. Includes FLOPS-adjusted counters to avoid over-weighting long-running kernels. (2) kvcache-offset-histogram: on-KVcache-access histogram recording offset distribution and small-window reuse counts to detect pathological stride patterns introduced by certain tokenizers ...

title: per-channel requantization + kernel fusion with KV-cache hinting to improve INT8/FP8 steady-state throughput
preview:
Title: Per-channel requantization + kernel fusion with KV-cache hinting to improve INT8/FP8 steady-state throughput

Summary / Motivation:
We observed that for real traffic profiles (small interactive requests with hot prefixes) the current INT8 execution path suffers from uneven per-head/per-channel scaling which r...
text:
Title: Per-channel requantization + kernel fusion with KV-cache hinting to improve INT8/FP8 steady-state throughput

Summary / Motivation:
We observed that for real traffic profiles (small interactive requests with hot prefixes) the current INT8 execution path suffers from uneven per-head/per-channel scaling which results in lower GEMM utilization and extra dequant/quant roundtrips around attention and MLP. This PR introduces a per-channel requantization stage inside the microkernel and fuses the dequant/scale operations into the attention + MLP kernels when safe. Additionally, it adds a lightweight KV-cache hint that allows the scheduler to prefer pre-warmed KV workers when requantization is detected, reducing cold-KV stalls for hot prefixes. The goal is to increase steady-state throughput without impacting worst-case correctness or model quality.

What changed (high level):
- Introd...

title: Attenuated warm-pool smoothing and utilization exposer for dedicated fleets
preview: Context: Dedicated fleet warm pools are currently susceptible to short-lived microbursts: a handful of tenants generate transient spikes that cause the warm-pool controller to over-allocate headroom, inflate cost, and produce noisy per-tenant utilization metrics. This PR introduces an attenuated smoothing policy for...
text: Context: Dedicated fleet warm pools are currently susceptible to short-lived microbursts: a handful of tenants generate transient spikes that cause the warm-pool controller to over-allocate headroom, inflate cost, and produce noisy per-tenant utilization metrics. This PR introduces an attenuated smoothing policy for warm-pool sizing and a lightweight utilization exposer that decouples instantaneous spikes from the long-running reservation logic. Motivation: reduce oscillation-driven overprovisioning, improve stability for tenant SLAs, and provide clearer telemetry to platform teams so they can tune reservations and burst contracts. Changes in this PR (high level): - Introduce an attenuated smoothing window with configurable decay factors and min/max headroom bounds. The algorithm applies exponential decay to short spikes while allowing sustained demand to grow warm pool capacity. - Ad...

title: Atomicize SSE chunk handshake and idempotent replay to fix backpressure edgecases
preview: Context: Customers reported rare production hangs when streaming long responses with function calls and structured JSON boundaries via the OpenAI-compat endpoint. Symptoms included stalled client streams when upstream frames were coalesced across network segments, duplicated trailing delimiters on reconnect, and occ...
text: Context: Customers reported rare production hangs when streaming long responses with function calls and structured JSON boundaries via the OpenAI-compat endpoint. Symptoms included stalled client streams when upstream frames were coalesced across network segments, duplicated trailing delimiters on reconnect, and occasional corrupt partial-JSON emitted to downstream consumers. Repro: long chat with streaming function_call followed by large JSON output and intermittent packet drops; reproduce with tests/sse/test_fragment_replay.py and a patched netem dropping 1/1000 packets. Root cause: two interacting issues: (1) the handshake/ack window between API gateway and runtime allowed non-atomic flushes where a delimiter byte could be emitted without its subsequent JSON tail when the downstream backpressure closed mid-chunk; (2) replay logic was non-idempotent for partially-applied fragments, ...

title: Introduce invocation affinity matrix and proactive alert resolver
preview: Motivation: We observe recurring noisy SLO alerts created by correlated bursts that cross tenant thresholds without reflecting distinct root causes. Current per-invocation alerts fire independently and cause paging fatigue. This PR adds a first-class invocation affinity metric and a proactive alert resolver that gro...
text:
Motivation: We observe recurring noisy SLO alerts created by correlated bursts that cross tenant thresholds without reflecting distinct root causes. Current per-invocation alerts fire independently and cause paging fatigue. This PR adds a first-class invocation affinity metric and a proactive alert resolver that groups related invocations into cohorts and suppresses/attenuates alerts when an upstream affinity signal indicates a single shared cause.

What this PR does: Adds a streaming affinity index computed at aggregation time (invocation_affinity{route, model, tenant, window}) derived from co-occurrence of invocation fingerprints, kernel contention signals, and token-phase overlap. Adds a Grafana panel to visualize the affinity matrix and an alert resolver that consumes the affinity index to decide alert coalescing and attenuation thresholds. The resolver is opt-in and governed by p...

title: add incident-capture CLI plus ephemeral playbook templates and safe artifact bundler
preview: Motivation: oncall responders repeatedly need a compact, reproducible snapshot of system state that is safe to share with SRE/eng. Existing runbook generators either produce too much PII or require interactive console steps. This PR introduces a lightweight CLI that: 1) collects a tunable set of diagnostics (logs, s...
text:
Motivation: oncall responders repeatedly need a compact, reproducible snapshot of system state that is safe to share with SRE/eng. Existing runbook generators either produce too much PII or require interactive console steps. This PR introduces a lightweight CLI that: 1) collects a tunable set of diagnostics (logs, system metrics, trace snippets, recent error samples), 2) performs automated redaction rules and entropy checks to avoid shipping secrets, and 3) packages artifacts into an ephemeral bundle with a reproducible hash and a short-lived presigned link for sharing. In addition, we include a small library of ephemeral playbook templates (network-isolation, safe-restart, rolling-back) that are parameterized and renderable by the incident-bot UI.

What changed (high level): added new binary `cmd/incident-capture` with subcommands `collect`, `redact`, `bundle`, `preview`; new Go pack...

title: introduce-lease-prefetch-and-adaptive-coalesce-to-reduce-streaming-stalls
preview: Motivation: Production traces show intermittent stalls (~30-120ms) at the streaming emission boundary during mixed small/large request workloads. Root cause analysis pointed to tight backpressure interactions between continuous microbatching and KV-cache cold walks: microbatches would wait for a minimal credit windo...
text:
Motivation: Production traces show intermittent stalls (~30-120ms) at the streaming emission boundary during mixed small/large request workloads. Root cause analysis pointed to tight backpressure interactions between continuous microbatching and KV-cache cold walks: microbatches would wait for a minimal credit window while concurrent large requests evicted warm KV pages, causing emission stalls when the next token attempt triggered a cache walk and kernel rebind. This PR introduces a lease-driven prefetch and an adaptive coalesce window in the emitter to smooth those interactions and reduce TTFB variance.

What this change does: 1) Lease-based KV prefetch: when a request starts streaming, it obtains a short-lived soft lease for recent KV ranges used in the request's prefix. The lease prevents immediate eviction and primes a background prefetch worker to warm KV pages asynchronously. L...

title: Hierarchy-aware traffic anchoring for deferred canaries with phase-aware rollback oracle
preview: Motivation: Large multi-service deployments require staged canaries that respect logical hierarchy and shared resources. This PR introduces a hierarchy-aware traffic anchoring controller that attaches canary phases to a stability anchor within a service hierarchy and uses phase-aware rollback heuristics to reduce no...
text: Motivation: Large multi-service deployments require staged canaries that respect logical hierarchy and shared resources. This PR introduces a hierarchy-aware traffic anchoring controller that attaches canary phases to a stability anchor within a service hierarchy and uses phase-aware rollback heuristics to reduce noisy rollbacks across sibling cohorts. Key design: 1) Anchor graph model representing parent-child service relationships and resource affinity to compute safe traffic slots for deferred canaries 2) Phase windows with per-phase safety budgets and decaying anomaly thresholds 3) Rollback-oracle that considers sibling stability, global SLO burn rate, and momentum of metrics to make automated rollback decisions 4) Graceful handoff: anchor holds traffic while downstream verifies session-consistent swaps and drain hooks. Backwards compatibility: default rollout behavior unchanged u...

title: Introduce kernel-level heatmap and cost-flow annotator for invocation traces
preview: Motivation: We need a low-overhead way to surface GPU/kernel-level queuing and contention hotspots correlated to token-level cost and per-stage latency. This PR adds a sampled, lightweight kernel-heatmap export and a cost-flow annotator that stitches stage timing with kernel occupancy to help SREs and runtime engine...
text: Motivation: We need a low-overhead way to surface GPU/kernel-level queuing and contention hotspots correlated to token-level cost and per-stage latency. This PR adds a sampled, lightweight kernel-heatmap export and a cost-flow annotator that stitches stage timing with kernel occupancy to help SREs and runtime engineers diagnose expensive invocation patterns. Design summary: 1) A new kernel heatmap sampler emits compact histograms of kernel queue wait and execution time per-device and per-invocation-phase; sampling is prioritized for requests that exceed a configurable latency or cost threshold. 2) A cost-flow annotator postprocesses traces to attach a per-invocation-stage cost attribution vector (model load, key-value cache miss penalties, kernel stalls, token-generation time) and synthesizes rollup markers used by dashboards. 3) Minimal on-path overhead: sampler uses atomic ring-buff...

title: Queue-horizon throttling and KV probe backoff for continuous batcher
preview: Motivation: recent production incidents showed pathological tail latency when short-lived high-priority traffic interleaved with KV-heavy long-context requests. The continuous batcher could become congested by probe/burst requests that pin KV slots and prevent fairness tokens from progressing, causing latency spikes...
text: Motivation: recent production incidents showed pathological tail latency when short-lived high-priority traffic interleaved with KV-heavy long-context requests. The continuous batcher could become congested by probe/burst requests that pin KV slots and prevent fairness tokens from progressing, causing latency spikes. This PR introduces a queue-horizon throttling policy and an exponential backoff for KV probe requests to reduce transient pin contention while preserving throughput.

title: Proportional backfill allocator and soft-reservation smoothing for Dedicated fleets
preview: Motivation: Dedicated fleets often have fragmented reserved capacity where tenants underutilize their reservations for short windows. Current behavior returns unused tokens aggressively which leads to oscillation when reservations are re-acquired and contributes to noisy autoscaler decisions. This PR implements a pr...
text:
Motivation: Dedicated fleets often have fragmented reserved capacity where tenants underutilize their reservations for short windows. Current behavior returns unused tokens aggressively which leads to oscillation when reservations are re-acquired and contributes to noisy autoscaler decisions. This PR implements a proportional backfill allocator plus soft-reservation smoothing to allow unused reserved capacity to be temporarily shared across tenants in a controlled, decaying manner.

What this changes (single string): 1) Proportional backfill allocator: when there is spare reserved headroom, we compute per-tenant proportional backfill based on their active demand and reservation weight. Backfill amounts are distributed as fractional credits with deterministic rounding to avoid starvation. Backfill is tagged with ttl and a reclaimable flag so original owners can reclaim capacity within ...

title: Hardening: entitlement sync job retries, idempotency keys, and SRE alert hooks
preview:
## Context
We’ve had intermittent marketplace onboarding/provisioning delays where a customer subscribes in AWS/GCP Marketplace but Redwood does not reflect entitlement quickly enough (or at all) due to transient upstream errors and non-idempotent reprocessing. This has shown up as:
- SUP-4821: entitlement/provision...
text:
## Context
We’ve had intermittent marketplace onboarding/provisioning delays where a customer subscribes in AWS/GCP Marketplace but Redwood does not reflect entitlement quickly enough (or at all) due to transient upstream errors and non-idempotent reprocessing. This has shown up as:
- SUP-4821: entitlement/provisioning delay after subscription (support escalation)
- SUP-4829: confusion from partial sync + billing dimension mismatch during replays

Root cause in recent cases: the entitlement sync worker treats some partner/API failures as terminal, and on retry/replay can double-emit "entitlement_granted" side effects (email/webhook + provisioning steps), leading to noisy logs and occasional inconsistent state.

## What’s in this PR
1) **Retries + backoff for upstream calls**
- Adds bounded exponential backoff with jitter for marketplace provider fetches (AWS entitlement lookup + GCP e...

title: Sessioned runbook orchestrator and alert seatbelt
preview: Context and motivation: On-call teams repeatedly asked for a way to bind ephemeral debugging sessions to runbooks so that responders and secondary engineers can join a single context (links, logs, temporary access) and avoid duplicate work. In parallel we needed a short-term noise-dampening mechanism (an "alert seat...
text: Context and motivation: On-call teams repeatedly asked for a way to bind ephemeral debugging sessions to runbooks so that responders and secondary engineers can join a single context (links, logs, temporary access) and avoid duplicate work. In parallel we needed a short-term noise-dampening mechanism (an "alert seatbelt") that automatically tempers noisy symptom alerts while keeping SLO-critical signals unsuppressed. This PR implements a sessioned runbook orchestrator plus an SLA-aware alert seatbelt feature.

title: Add marketplace SKU IDs to billing catalog + map to metering dimensions (v2)
preview:
## Context
We’re refreshing AWS + GCP marketplace listings and moving to a new SKU catalog (Hosted/Dedicated/Private + add-ons). To support clean procurement paths and reduce onboarding/billing friction, the core billing catalog needs stable marketplace-facing SKU identifiers and an explicit mapping from marketplace...
text:
## Context
We’re refreshing AWS + GCP marketplace listings and moving to a new SKU catalog (Hosted/Dedicated/Private + add-ons). To support clean procurement paths and reduce onboarding/billing friction, the core billing catalog needs stable marketplace-facing SKU identifiers and an explicit mapping from marketplace dimensions -> internal meters.

This PR adds first-class `marketplace_sku_id` entries to the billing catalog and defines the authoritative mapping to internal meter keys used by the usage pipeline.

## What changed
- Adds `marketplace_sku_id` to billing catalog entries for all marketplace-eligible products (Hosted, Dedicated, Private, and add-ons).
- Introduces a `marketplace_dimension_mapping` table that maps:
 - provider (`aws` | `gcp`)
 - marketplace dimension name/id
 - Redwood `marketplace_sku_id`
 - internal meter key (what we actually record against)
- Adds validati...

title: Add tiered batching profile schema (fast/standard/cost-efficient) with validation + backcompat
preview: Summary: This PR introduces the config schema and validation needed to ship tiered batching defaults (fast / standard / cost-efficient) while keeping backwards compatibility with existing per-route batching knobs. This is plumbing-only: it defines how tiers and optional per-tier profile overrides are expressed, vali...
text: Summary: This PR introduces the config schema and validation needed to ship tiered batching defaults (fast / standard / cost-efficient) while keeping backwards compatibility with existing per-route batching knobs. This is plumbing-only: it defines how tiers and optional per-tier profile overrides are expressed, validated, and surfaced to runtime config resolution. It does not change scheduler heuristics or default knob values; those land in follow-up PRs. Context: As part of workload-aware batching defaults, we need a stable, customer-facing tier concept that can be selected at the route/model policy level and overridden by enterprise/Dedicated configs. We also need to ensure customers with existing manual batching settings do not break, and that invalid or partially-specified configs fail fast with actionable error messages. What’s included: (1) New schema types: - Adds a new enum `B...

title: Introduce variant-weighted phased gate and explainable failure reporter
preview: Motivation: We need a gate that can phase-in stricter regression checks across multiple prompt-set variants and weight them by production traffic mix. Existing gates are either binary or treat variants equally, which causes noisy failures from low-impact variants and slow rollouts when a single high-volume variant c...
text:
Motivation: We need a gate that can phase-in stricter regression checks across multiple prompt-set variants and weight them by production traffic mix. Existing gates are either binary or treat variants equally, which causes noisy failures from low-impact variants and slow rollouts when a single high-volume variant changes. This PR implements a phased "variant-weighted" quality gate plus an explainable failure reporter that surfaces per-variant contribution and suggested mitigations.

Summary of changes: added a PhaseGate orchestration that accepts variant weights and stage thresholds, a scoring adapter that produces per-variant metrics (accuracy, token-loss, semantic-distance), and an explainability module that generates a short failure report with example prompts, model outputs, and a concise mitigation recommendation. The gate supports three phases (shadow, canary, enforce) and auto...

title: Concurrent burst decoupler and safety thresholds for reservation subsystem
preview: Motivation: In high-concurrency scenarios tenants were simultaneously consuming burst credits and driving reservation drains that caused transient SLA drops. Existing soft-reserve logic treated burst tokens and concurrency as a single coupled signal which amplified scale-down decisions. This PR introduces a concurre...
text:
Motivation: In high-concurrency scenarios tenants were simultaneously consuming burst credits and driving reservation drains that caused transient SLA drops. Existing soft-reserve logic treated burst tokens and concurrency as a single coupled signal which amplified scale-down decisions. This PR introduces a concurrent-burst decoupler and tenant safety thresholds to ensure reservation drains are gated by both credit state and observed concurrency pressure.

Summary of changes: This is a focused, backend-only change that touches the reservation allocator, autoscaler decision path, and telemetry surface. Key pieces: 1) decoupler layer that separates short-lived concurrent bursts from sustained reservation usage; 2) tenant safety thresholds (min-reserved-headroom and concurrency-fence) which temporarily block aggressive shrink decisions; 3) util-backpressure estimator that reports a stabi...

title: shifted telemetry compressor and humanized handoff note generator
preview: Motivation: On-call handoffs often arrive with large telemetry attachments and ambiguous next steps. This PR introduces a compact telemetry compressor and a humanized handoff note generator to reduce payload size, remove PII, and provide a cognitive-load estimate and a canary-safe rollback playbook seeded from the c...
text: Motivation: On-call handoffs often arrive with large telemetry attachments and ambiguous next steps. This PR introduces a compact telemetry compressor and a humanized handoff note generator to reduce payload size, remove PII, and provide a cognitive-load estimate and a canary-safe rollback playbook seeded from the collected snapshot. The snapshot collector produces a small 'repro trailer' containing minimal trace slices, top-k logs by error signature, sampled heap/disk metadata and a KV diff bundle. The compressor uses a delta-based rolling window (inspired by rsync-style chunking) plus optional gzip fallback to guarantee size and decompression determinism across platforms. The handoff note generator templates the critical actions, suggested playbook steps, and a short 'operator confidence' metric based on recent similar incidents and successful mitigations. Implementation: adds a new...

title: Deprecate legacy stream shims; add credential lookup facade and unify SDK iterables
preview: Context: Multiple SDK consumers reported subtle differences in auth lookup timing and streaming iterator shapes across languages. The legacy 'stream shims' layer introduced in earlier rollouts has become a maintenance burden and makes cross-language parity work harder. Motivation: Reduce duplicated auth lookup code,...
text: Context: Multiple SDK consumers reported subtle differences in auth lookup timing and streaming iterator shapes across languages. The legacy 'stream shims' layer introduced in earlier rollouts has become a maintenance burden and makes cross-language parity work harder. Motivation: Reduce duplicated auth lookup code, surface a single lightweight facade for credential resolution that all SDKs call into, and simplify streaming APIs to a single async iterator/pull-generator mapping with clearer backpressure hints. What this PR does: 1) Adds a Credential Lookup Facade (sdk/common/cred_lookup) that centralizes token resolution, TTL jitter logic, and prefetch hints used by the Python, TypeScript, and Go SDKs. 2) Reworks streaming surface so each SDK exports a single ergonomic async/pull iterator that maps to the runtime streaming protocol; legacy shims (StreamShimV1) are deprecated and left ...

title: Introduce deferred-KV-eviction + quant-tier orchestrator and tenant econ profiles
preview: Motivation: long-tail conversational traffic and multi-tenant variability mean the KV cache is frequently evicted from mini-batches even though reusing older KV fragments would save tokens and improve unit economics. Separately, tenant-facing quantization defaults are too coarse: some tenants can accept int8 for bac...
text: Motivation: long-tail conversational traffic and multi-tenant variability mean the KV cache is frequently evicted from mini-batches even though reusing older KV fragments would save tokens and improve unit economics. Separately, tenant-facing quantization defaults are too coarse: some tenants can accept int8 for background low-SLO routes while others require fp16 for latency-sensitive chat. This PR adds two coordinated pieces: 1) a deferred-KV-eviction policy in the serving runtime that postpones eager KV eviction for low-probability prefix reuse and exposes eviction scoring for per-tenant policies; 2) a quant-tier-orchestrator service + CLI that evaluates unit-econ vs latency tradeoffs and dynamically routes tenants to quantization tiers (fp16, int8, int4-probative) with safety fallbacks. Changes include a lightweight controller that ingests tenant cost/latency targets and historical...

title: Introduce stream-cadence fingerprint and cost mirroring for observability
preview: Context and motivation: customer incidents and internal SLO runs showed repeated small latency drifts during streaming responses that were not captured by existing per-request percentiles. These drifts correlate with bursty token emission patterns (short, frequent microbursts) and cross-tenant billing anomalies. Thi...
text:
Context and motivation: customer incidents and internal SLO runs showed repeated small latency drifts during streaming responses that were not captured by existing per-request percentiles. These drifts correlate with bursty token emission patterns (short, frequent microbursts) and cross-tenant billing anomalies. This PR introduces two complementary observability primitives: (1) a stream-cadence fingerprint metric that summarizes emission cadence per invocation, and (2) a cost-mirroring pipeline that writes compact per-invocation cost samples to the billing index for reconciliation and anomaly detection.

What I changed (summary):
- Added a lightweight stream-cadence fingerprint generator in src/observability/stream_cadence/fingerprint.go which computes a 64-bit sketch (hash-bucketed inter-token delta histogram) and emits a low-cardinality label (cadence_bucket) + discrete features as ...

title: unified tool-caller API + delayed-commit retry semantics to stabilize streaming and multi-tool results
preview: Context and motivation: Customers frequently surface two pain points when composing LLM responses with external tools: 1) streaming instability when tool results and model tokens interleave mid-stream, and 2) duplicate side-effects from naive retry semantics for parallel tool calls. This PR introduces a unified 'Too...
text: Context and motivation: Customers frequently surface two pain points when composing LLM responses with external tools: 1) streaming instability when tool results and model tokens interleave mid-stream, and 2) duplicate side-effects from naive retry semantics for parallel tool calls. This PR introduces a unified 'Tool Caller' API and a delayed-commit retry strategy to address both issues in a developer-friendly, OpenAI-compatible surface. Design summary: The Tool Caller provides a single entrypoint for: a) orchestrating ordered or parallel tool invocations with deterministic merge semantics, b) registering streaming checkpoints so the model can safely stream partial token output without observing incomplete tool side-effects, and c) delayed-commit retries that only expose tool results to the model after idempotency checks pass. Streaming stability is handled via per-tool stream checkpo...

title: introduce-path-aware-context-anchors-and-bloom-log-summaries
preview:
Motivation:
We see a recurring pattern where SRE and oncall engineers spend time hunting long-tail incidents that are noisy because traces lack compact contextual anchors. Full log payloads increase cardinality in downstream indices and make alert grouping brittle. This PR introduces a compact, path-aware context an...
text:
Motivation:
We see a recurring pattern where SRE and oncall engineers spend time hunting long-tail incidents that are noisy because traces lack compact contextual anchors. Full log payloads increase cardinality in downstream indices and make alert grouping brittle. This PR introduces a compact, path-aware context anchor service that produces bloom-filter based context summaries (compact anchors) for invocations and attaches them to traces, metrics, and synthetic telemetry. The anchors enable lightweight contextual joins in dashboards and reduce alert noise by enabling fuzzy grouping and de-duplication.

What this PR does (high-level):
- New component: observability-context-bridge (Go microservice) that consumes span events, generates a 128-bit bloom-ish anchor and a short context digest (max 128 bytes) using deterministic keying of sanitized prompt tokens and route path.
- Runtime: a ...

title: introduce-cohort-emulator-with-flame-segmentation-reporter
preview: Context: We often see p95/p99 spikes in the benchmark lab but lack a compact, deterministic way to map tail episodes to kernel-level hotspots across cohorts. This PR adds a lightweight cohort emulator that replays compact multi-session cohorts with controlled timing and a new flame-segmentation reporter that emits c...
text: Context: We often see p95/p99 spikes in the benchmark lab but lack a compact, deterministic way to map tail episodes to kernel-level hotspots across cohorts. This PR adds a lightweight cohort emulator that replays compact multi-session cohorts with controlled timing and a new flame-segmentation reporter that emits compact 'flame segment' artifacts for downstream attribution and visualization. Motivation: Make it possible to reproduce microburst segments deterministically, compare them against a golden set, and quickly iterate on kernel/serving changes without full cluster replays. Scope: emulator core, segmentation reporter, goldset generator, CI perf job integration, docs and an example cohort. Checklist: - [x] Emulator core with deterministic seed and time dilation - [x] Flame-segmentation reporter that emits compact JSON segments and optional pprof links - [x] Goldset generator and...

title: decouple-serializer-from-affinity-and-introduce-cooperative-serialize-queues
preview: Motivation: our telemetry and recent incident traces show a non-trivial CPU-side bottleneck in the request hotpath caused by blocking serialization work that holds thread-affinity (OS-level pinning) and prevents efficient cooperative scheduling between IO, serializer, and runtime workers. This PR separates the conce...
text: Motivation: our telemetry and recent incident traces show a non-trivial CPU-side bottleneck in the request hotpath caused by blocking serialization work that holds thread-affinity (OS-level pinning) and prevents efficient cooperative scheduling between IO, serializer, and runtime workers. This PR separates the concerns: (1) introduce a lightweight cooperative serialization queue that runs on worker threads without requiring permanent thread pinning, (2) decouple the serializer state machine from thread-affinity so short critical sections can opportunistically run on any available worker, and (3) add backpressure signals to the queue which feed into the scheduler to dynamically throttle batch assembly when CPU saturation is detected. Design summary: add runtime/serialize_queue.{h,cpp} implementing a fixed-size ring queue with lock-free handover for small payloads and a CAS-protected ov...

title: Epochal frontier admission prober for soft-horizon continuous batching
preview: Summary: Implements an 'Epochal Frontier Admission Prober' (EAP) for the continuous batching scheduler to reduce starvation for KV-heavy and short-lived requests while preserving throughput for long sequences. Motivation: we've observed recurring tail latency regressions under mixed workloads when aggressive prefix ...
text: Summary: Implements an 'Epochal Frontier Admission Prober' (EAP) for the continuous batching scheduler to reduce starvation for KV-heavy and short-lived requests while preserving throughput for long sequences. Motivation: we've observed recurring tail latency regressions under mixed workloads when aggressive prefix caching and long batching windows are enabled. Existing fixes (shorter windows, conservative admission limits) either hurt throughput or increase cost. EAP introduces a lightweight, epoch-based admission probe that temporarily lowers admission thresholds for requests at the queue frontier, with jittered short leases and a soft-horizon window to prevent repeated preemption. The design is conservative: probes are sampled per-epoch and back off exponentially on contention.

title: scheduler: jittered slot reassignment and KV-warmup throttling
preview: Context: In high-concurrency mixed workloads we observed correlated slot reclamations and KV cache warmups causing transient starvation and p95 latency spikes. This PR implements two complementary runtime controls: (1) jittered slot reassignment to spread slot release/reassignment events across short randomized wind...
text: Context: In high-concurrency mixed workloads we observed correlated slot reclamations and KV cache warmups causing transient starvation and p95 latency spikes. This PR implements two complementary runtime controls: (1) jittered slot reassignment to spread slot release/reassignment events across short randomized windows (configurable), and (2) a KV-warmup throttler that staggers cold-KV prefetches and enforces a soft admission budget per-slot to avoid simultaneous heavy prefetches. Changes: Implements a new scheduler helper (jitter_reassign) which inserts small randomized offsets before slot handoff and coordinates with the slot allocator to avoid churn; implements kv_warmup_throttle which tracks per-model warmup tokens and decays budgets based on successful cache hits; exposes scheduler.tuning.jitter_ms and kv.warmup.budget_rate config knobs; updates continuous_batcher admission path ...

title: token-span-attribution-and-adaptive-alert-smoothing
preview:
Motivation:
We currently surface token-level cost and latency aggregations but lack a clear way to attribute SLO impact to specific token spans (e.g. model prompt prefix vs generated tail) and we get frequent alert flapping on percentile SLOs when low-volume, high-cost tails show up. This PR introduces two complemen...
text:
Motivation:
We currently surface token-level cost and latency aggregations but lack a clear way to attribute SLO impact to specific token spans (e.g. model prompt prefix vs generated tail) and we get frequent alert flapping on percentile SLOs when low-volume, high-cost tails show up. This PR introduces two complementary capabilities: 1) span-level token attribution traces and rollups keyed by token position buckets (prefix/mid/suffix) and cache-affinity (kv-hit vs kv-miss); and 2) an adaptive alert-smoothing layer that reduces noise for route-level token-cost SLOs using a decayed-reservoir quantile estimator that weights tokens by cost and recency.

What this changes (high level):
- Runtime telemetry: emit token-level spans with attributes: token_index, span_bucket (prefix|mid|suffix), kv_affinity (hit|miss), token_cost_units. These are synthesized as aggregated metrics and lightweigh...

title: Introduce invocation segmentation + weighted-error suppressor and segment-level cost/latency metrics
preview: Motivation: We frequently see noisy error spikes from downstream adapters and third-party integrations that cause alert storming and obscure true regressions. At the same time, token-level cost/latency rollups miss stagewise behavior inside long-running invocations (e.g., prefetch, model-run, post-process). This PR ...
text:
Motivation: We frequently see noisy error spikes from downstream adapters and third-party integrations that cause alert storming and obscure true regressions. At the same time, token-level cost/latency rollups miss stagewise behavior inside long-running invocations (e.g., prefetch, model-run, post-process). This PR adds an invocation segmentation layer that (1) marks logical stages within an invocation, (2) emits segment-scoped metrics (latency, token-cost, errors), and (3) implements a lightweight weighted-error suppressor so transient noisy components don't overwhelm on-call channels.

Summary of changes: added a runtime segmenter used by the serving pipeline, new metrics (invocation_segment_latency_seconds_bucket, invocation_segment_token_cost_cents, invocation_segment_error_weight), tracing span naming for segments (invocation.segment.{name}), log enrichment with segment_id and se...

title: Introduce sequence-entropy alignment and token billing lens
preview: Motivation: Many SRE and billing investigations require correlating the information-theoretic "sequence entropy" signature of a generation request with the downstream billed token cost and latency profile. Existing observability surfaces show per-token cost and coarse prompt shape metrics but do not make it easy to ...
text:
Motivation: Many SRE and billing investigations require correlating the information-theoretic "sequence entropy" signature of a generation request with the downstream billed token cost and latency profile. Existing observability surfaces show per-token cost and coarse prompt shape metrics but do not make it easy to identify when high-entropy sequences are driving disproportionate billing or SLO pressure. This PR introduces a backend metric pipeline and a user-facing dashboard (the 'billing lens') that aligns per-sequence entropy, token-cost attribution, and latency heatmaps so teams can triage high-cost tails faster.

What this change does (single-line description): adds sequence-entropy aggregator, normalized per-tenant cost correlation metric, a Grafana lens dashboard, alert tuning for entropy-driven cost drift, and a small SDK helper for telemetry tagging.

Scope & components chang...

title: Headroom orchestration and differential burst accountant
preview: Motivation: production tenants exhibit asymmetric load patterns (short microbursts vs sustained ramps). Existing global headroom and simple burst tokens over/under-react, causing either wasted reserved capacity or SLA violations during coordinated microbursts. This PR implements a new headroom orchestration layer th...
text:
Motivation: production tenants exhibit asymmetric load patterns (short microbursts vs sustained ramps). Existing global headroom and simple burst tokens over/under-react, causing either wasted reserved capacity or SLA violations during coordinated microbursts. This PR implements a new headroom orchestration layer that computes per-tenant asymmetric headroom weights and a separate differential burst accountant that decouples short-window burst credits from long-window commitment accounting.

Design summary: the headroom orchestrator consumes sampled utilization histograms from tenant reporters and produces two outputs: (1) dynamic short-window headroom multiplier (reacts to 95th/99th percentile short histograms), and (2) steady long-window buffer (based on 90th percentile over longer horizon). The differential burst accountant maintains two credit buckets per tenant: ephemeral credits ...

title: Introduce presubmit cost sampler and toolchain stabilizer
preview: Context: CI noise from transitive dep updates and toolchain version flips has led to intermittent presubmit failures and unpredictable cost spikes in hosted runs. Motivation: reduce noise, gather sample-based cost telemetry, and stabilize the dev toolchain so presubmit failures are actionable rather than transient. ...
text: Context: CI noise from transitive dep updates and toolchain version flips has led to intermittent presubmit failures and unpredictable cost spikes in hosted runs. Motivation: reduce noise, gather sample-based cost telemetry, and stabilize the dev toolchain so presubmit failures are actionable rather than transient. What this PR does: 1) Adds a lightweight presubmit-cost-sampler pipeline (ci/presubmit/sampler.yaml) that runs on ~0.5% of PRs and records job-level time/cpu/mem/infra-cost estimates to the internal CI-telemetry store. 2) Introduces a toolchain-stabilizer service (infra/toolchain-stabilizer/) that: - pins a curated minimal set of tool versions for presubmit lanes, - warms language/OS-specific caches on ephemeral runners, - injects a cache-warm header into existing install steps. 3) Adds an opt-in "quiet-merge-window" gating policy (scripts/merge-window/README.md) that batch...

title: Introduce policy simulator with KMS proofing and audit-diff tooling
preview: Title: Introduce policy simulator with KMS proofing and audit-diff tooling. Motivation: Customers and internal auditors frequently ask for a way to validate RBAC v2 policy changes before rollout and to produce cryptographic proof that a proposed change does not widen region/KMS residency exposure. This PR adds a dev...
text: Title: Introduce policy simulator with KMS proofing and audit-diff tooling. Motivation: Customers and internal auditors frequently ask for a way to validate RBAC v2 policy changes before rollout and to produce cryptographic proof that a proposed change does not widen region/KMS residency exposure. This PR adds a developer-facing simulator (rbac-sim) that evaluates a set of example requests against a candidate policy delta, emits a human- and machine-readable audit-diff, and (optionally) attaches a short-lived KMS-signed proof bundle that attests to the simulator inputs and outputs for downstream compliance workflows. This is intended to reduce post-deploy surprises, speed review cycles for Dedicated/Private deployments, and plug into our audit retention processes. Summary of changes (high level commits): 1) feat(simulator): introduce rbac-sim CLI and library with JSON policy delta app...

title: Introduce contextualized minibatch regression watchdog and suppression adapter
preview: Motivation: Flaky per-prompt regressions are noisy in CI when single minibatch failures trigger rollbacks or high-severity alerts. This PR adds a contextualized minibatch regression watchdog that computes short-horizon, context-aware regression signals and a suppression adapter that dampens alerts from transient min...
text:
Motivation: Flaky per-prompt regressions are noisy in CI when single minibatch failures trigger rollbacks or high-severity alerts. This PR adds a contextualized minibatch regression watchdog that computes short-horizon, context-aware regression signals and a suppression adapter that dampens alerts from transient minibatch noise. The goal is to reduce false positives in CI gating while preserving sensitivity to sustained quality regressions.

What this PR introduces: 1) src/watchdogs/contextual_minibatch_watchdog.py - new watchdog which calculates per-prompt-context rolling statistics (short window mean, variance, exponential smoothing) and emits a scored regression signal. 2) src/adapters/suppression_adapter.py - a pluggable adapter used in CI and alerting pipelines to apply hysteresis and suppression rules: confirmation windows, adaptive thresholds based on context prevalence, and a ...

title: Add binding inheritance guard and geo-key provenance header
preview: Summary: This PR adds a binding inheritance guard to RBACv2, introduces a lightweight KMS provenance header (X-KMS-Origin-Chain) surfaced through the gateway, and a small audit consolidator used by the audit-forwarder to deduplicate events coming from chained proxies. Motivation: We observed two related classes of c...
text: Summary: This PR adds a binding inheritance guard to RBACv2, introduces a lightweight KMS provenance header (X-KMS-Origin-Chain) surfaced through the gateway, and a small audit consolidator used by the audit-forwarder to deduplicate events coming from chained proxies. Motivation: We observed two related classes of compliance gaps in multi-zone customers: (1) operator/service bindings that unintentionally inherited privileges when tenant objects were copied across geozone boundaries and (2) duplicated/misaligned audit entries from edge proxies that obscured key provenance. The changes here are intended to close those gaps with minimal disruption and a staged rollout. What changed (high level): - rbac/policy-compiler: add BindingInheritanceGuard module. The guard enforces that operator/service role bindings cannot be transitively inherited across geozone boundaries unless an explicit 'i...

title: introduce-historical-variance-detector-and-variance-gate-hook
preview: Motivation: We've seen intermittent metric oscillations across repeated eval runs (same prompt set, different seeds / sampling windows) that cause nondeterministic gate failures and noisy postdeploy regressions. This PR introduces a Historical Variance Detector (HVD) to detect when a prompt-set or cohort exhibits un...
text:
Motivation: We've seen intermittent metric oscillations across repeated eval runs (same prompt set, different seeds / sampling windows) that cause nondeterministic gate failures and noisy postdeploy regressions. This PR introduces a Historical Variance Detector (HVD) to detect when a prompt-set or cohort exhibits unstable score distributions over a sliding window of previous eval runs and optionally block merges.

What I changed (high level):
- Add a new module eval_harness/variance_detector.py that computes per-prompt and cohort-level variance, runs a two-sample KS test against the historical distribution, and produces a compact variance report.
- Add an opt-in GH Action workflow .github/workflows/variance-gate.yml that invokes the harness check with the new --variance-check flag on PRs targeting main and release/*.
- Extend the CLI: `harness check --suite <suite> --variance-check --...

title: Cost‑Smarts: telemetry-driven prefill scheduler and quantization hint adapter
preview: Context and motivation: Large parts of our cost-automation stack rely on static prefill and quantization defaults that are tuned per-model but not per-workload. Customers running mixed telemetry (short chat bursts + long completion bursts) see suboptimal unit economics because prefill and quant decisions are not bei...
text:
Context and motivation: Large parts of our cost-automation stack rely on static prefill and quantization defaults that are tuned per-model but not per-workload. Customers running mixed telemetry (short chat bursts + long completion bursts) see suboptimal unit economics because prefill and quant decisions are not being informed by runtime signals. This PR introduces 'Cost‑Smarts' — a telemetry-driven prefill scheduler plus a quantization hint adapter that surfaces unit-econ deltas into the serving runtime policy engine.

What this change does: Implements a small, runtime-side scheduler that consumes existing request telemetry (sequence length histograms, prefix-kv hit ratios, tail-latency percentile) and emits short-lived prefill decisions (aggressive/standard/disabled) for a given route+model tuple. In parallel, the quantization hint adapter exposes a per-route score representing the ...

title: tenant-latency-bucketed-reservation-and-surge-thresholds
preview: Summary: Adds a tenant-level latency-bucketed reservation system and a configurable surge threshold gate to Dedicated fleet admission and the autoscaler pipeline. The change introduces a small, opt-in admission controller that tracks recent latency percentiles per-tenant, maps requests into latency buckets (p0, p50,...
text:
Summary: Adds a tenant-level latency-bucketed reservation system and a configurable surge threshold gate to Dedicated fleet admission and the autoscaler pipeline. The change introduces a small, opt-in admission controller that tracks recent latency percentiles per-tenant, maps requests into latency buckets (p0, p50, p90, p99), and enforces soft reservations per-bucket. When a tenant exceeds its budgeted bucket, requests are conditionally routed to a surge queue with rate-limited admission; if the surge queue fills, requests are gracefully degraded or routed to an overflow model variant based on policy.

Motivation: We observed repeated incidents where single-tenant tail latency spikes (p99) triggered autoscaler upsizing and burst credits consumption while average-throughput remained low. This caused suboptimal capacity churn and degraded multi-tenant fairness. The latency-bucket model...

title: Signal router: add core metrics + dashboard hooks (created_signals, routed_tasks, HubSpot errors, lag)
preview: Context: For the PLG-to-sales signal router, we need first-class visibility into end-to-end health (signal creation volume, HubSpot routing volume, HubSpot error rates, and pipeline lag) before expanding the pilot cohort. This PR instruments the signal-router service with a small set of stable metrics and wires them...
text: Context: For the PLG-to-sales signal router, we need first-class visibility into end-to-end health (signal creation volume, HubSpot routing volume, HubSpot error rates, and pipeline lag) before expanding the pilot cohort. This PR instruments the signal-router service with a small set of stable metrics and wires them into our dashboards/alerts scaffolding so Oncall + BizOps can see regressions quickly. Linked: ENG-7824 (dashboards/alerts for pipeline health). Related: INT-1843 (HubSpot 429s blocking task creation). What’s in this PR: (1) Service metrics (Prometheus): created_signals_total: counter; incremented when we persist a new PLG Signal (post-dedupe) (labels: env, signal_type, cohort). routed_tasks_total: counter; incremented on successful HubSpot task creation (labels: env, task_type, signal_type). hubspot_429s_total: counter; incremented when HubSpot responds 429 (labels: env, ...

title: Add signal suppression + false-positive guardrails (internal org filtering, min-volume gates)
preview: Context: During the NA pilot we’re seeing a non-trivial number of “vanity” intent triggers and a couple of true false positives (notably Quartz Commerce) resulting in unwanted outreach (see SUP-3392). This PR adds a set of guardrails to reduce noise while keeping recall for genuinely sales-worthy signals. Summary of...
text: Context: During the NA pilot we’re seeing a non-trivial number of “vanity” intent triggers and a couple of true false positives (notably Quartz Commerce) resulting in unwanted outreach (see SUP-3392). This PR adds a set of guardrails to reduce noise while keeping recall for genuinely sales-worthy signals. Summary of changes: (1) Suppression windows: prevent re-routing the same signal type for the same account within a configurable cooldown (default 14d for intent, 7d for reliability/usage) unless severity increases. We store last_routed_at by (workspace_id, signal_type) and compare against current signal_score + severity. (2) Internal/test org filtering: blocklist/allowlist logic for Redwood-owned workspaces and known test tenants; also filters “example.com” style seeded domains and sandbox workspaces tagged by billing. This is applied before scoring so we avoid polluting analytics ta...

title: Route qualified PLG signals to HubSpot tasks + timeline notes
preview: Context: We now have daily aggregated + scored PLG signals in the analytics table and a HubSpot client wrapper with retries/backoff (PR #28503). This PR wires the “qualified signal” output into HubSpot so AEs/SDRs can act on it in the pilot without needing to check internal dashboards. Scope for MVP: (1) Upsert a sm...
text: Context: We now have daily aggregated + scored PLG signals in the analytics table and a HubSpot client wrapper with retries/backoff (PR #28503). This PR wires the “qualified signal” output into HubSpot so AEs/SDRs can act on it in the pilot without needing to check internal dashboards. Scope for MVP: (1) Upsert a small set of company properties on the matched HubSpot Company record (last signal type/time/score + short summary + a rolling count), (2) append a timeline note containing a human-readable summary + stable machine markers for dedupe/audit, (3) create a follow-up task using the pilot task template, and (4) tag the signal type on both the note and task. This is intentionally conservative: we do not create or enroll contacts in sequences automatically; we only create tasks/notes and preserve existing ownership routing (ownership selection lives in ADR + ENG-7750 pipeline). Link...

title: Add PLG Signal schema + event contract (v1) with validation + versioning
preview: Summary: This PR introduces the canonical PLG-to-sales “Signal” schema (v1) and the upstream event contract used by the aggregation/scoring pipeline. It includes protobuf + JSON schema definitions, a small validation library, and versioning conventions so downstream systems (aggregator + HubSpot router) can evolve s...
text:
Summary: This PR introduces the canonical PLG-to-sales “Signal” schema (v1) and the upstream event contract used by the aggregation/scoring pipeline. It includes protobuf + JSON schema definitions, a small validation library, and versioning conventions so downstream systems (aggregator + HubSpot router) can evolve safely. This is the implementation referenced by PM-482 and ADR: usage/intent signals schema v1.

Context / motivation: We need a stable, privacy-aware contract for “sales-worthy” product-led signals (threshold + intent) so that: (1) telemetry/analytics can aggregate reliably across daily/weekly windows, (2) dedupe behavior is deterministic across backfills, and (3) routing systems can operate on a consistent set of fields/enums without embedding product-specific logic in every consumer.

What’s included: - New schema package: protos/plg/signal/v1/signal.proto defining Signa...

title: Implement daily PLG signal aggregation job (windowing, backfills, idempotent writes)
preview: Summary: Implements the daily aggregation job for PLG-to-sales signals. The job rolls up raw product telemetry into daily and trailing-window aggregates and writes the results to a partitioned analytics table for downstream scoring/routing. This is the pipeline piece for ENG-7732 (aggregation + windowing + backfill ...
text:
Summary: Implements the daily aggregation job for PLG-to-sales signals. The job rolls up raw product telemetry into daily and trailing-window aggregates and writes the results to a partitioned analytics table for downstream scoring/routing. This is the pipeline piece for ENG-7732 (aggregation + windowing + backfill + idempotency) and is designed to be safe to rerun.

Context / Why: The signal router needs a stable, privacy-aware, low-noise view of usage + intent inputs over consistent time windows. Raw event volume is high and late-arriving events are expected. This PR introduces a daily batch that produces canonical aggregates keyed by (org_id, workspace_id, signal_namespace, metric_name, window_start_date) and supports deterministic reprocessing.

What’s included (MVP): (1) Airflow DAG scheduled daily (UTC) with configurable lookback + late-event grace period. (2) Windowing helpers ...

title: Hotfix: disable INT8 profile on model variant x1 after quality regression
preview: Context: During the Quantization Profile Library beta, Applied ML flagged a statistically significant regression on the Redwood internal prompt set ("support_summaries_v2") when enabling profile `qp_int8_calib_v3` on model variant `x1` (model id `rw-lm/atlas-7b`, variant `x1`). This aligns with a design partner repo...
text:
Context: During the Quantization Profile Library beta, Applied ML flagged a statistically significant regression on the Redwood internal prompt set ("support_summaries_v2") when enabling profile `qp_int8_calib_v3` on model variant `x1` (model id `rw-lm/atlas-7b`, variant `x1`). This aligns with a design partner report of output drift after enablement (see SUP-27411). We performed an immediate operational rollback by disabling the profile at the affected route(s) and verified recovery, but we also need a hard guardrail to prevent re-enable while we recalibrate/re-evaluate.

Incident references: Slack #incidents thread 1741684000 (quality regression alert during beta) and #eng-ml thread 1740068833 (bench + regression notes). This PR implements a targeted hotfix in the control-plane/policy layer so the profile cannot be applied for `atlas-7b:x1` even if referenced in route config. Runtim...

title: Add global kill-switch config for quantization profiles (routing + runtime propagation)
preview: Summary: Introduces a platform-level kill-switch to disable Quantization Profile application globally, regardless of per-route settings. This is intended for emergency response (quality regression, incompatibility bug, or unexpected runtime instability) and is designed to be deterministic, auditable, and fast to pro...
text:
Summary: Introduces a platform-level kill-switch to disable Quantization Profile application globally, regardless of per-route settings. This is intended for emergency response (quality regression, incompatibility bug, or unexpected runtime instability) and is designed to be deterministic, auditable, and fast to propagate.

Context/Motivation: Route-level enablement is the default control for v1, but we need a single knob for SRE/oncall to quickly halt profile application without editing individual route configs. This PR adds a config flag that is evaluated in the control-plane resolution path and propagated through routing to the runtime. When the kill-switch is active, the resolved quantization outcome is forced to baseline and we record a consistent reason code.

Design goals: (1) Default behavior unchanged when kill-switch is off. (2) Deterministic: the same request under the same...
links: ADR-0137 (route-level enablement) referenced for non-goal: not changing route semantics
Internal runbook: quant-profiles-rollback.md (updated in this PR)

title: Policy engine: gate evaluation v1 for Dedicated staged rollouts (latency/error/saturation)
preview:
This PR integrates gate evaluation into the policy engine for Dedicated staged rollouts. v1 supports the core SRE-aligned signals (error rate, latency, saturation/headroom) and defines deterministic evaluation windows with safe behavior under missing/lagging telemetry.

Context
- Dedicated staged rollouts introduce ...
text:
This PR integrates gate evaluation into the policy engine for Dedicated staged rollouts. v1 supports the core SRE-aligned signals (error rate, latency, saturation/headroom) and defines deterministic evaluation windows with safe behavior under missing/lagging telemetry.

Context
- Dedicated staged rollouts introduce explicit stage promotion gated on objective signals.
- The policy engine is the single place we can enforce consistent behavior across Console actions (promote) and automated controllers (auto-promote/auto-abort).
- This PR implements the evaluation primitives + default query templates; it intentionally does NOT attempt to be a general experimentation framework.

What’s included
- GateEvaluationService in policy-engine:
 - `EvaluateDedicatedPoolStageGates(pool_id, rollout_id, stage_id, at_time)` returns a stable decision: PASS | FAIL | UNKNOWN, plus structured reasons.
 - D...
